markdown_projection.note: JSON contract is authority. No markdown projection is generated for WP-KERNEL-009 (prevents projection drift).

scope.scope: Separate raw document content, derived summaries/previews, and display-only UI state. This MT belongs to RichDocumentCore. Build the canonical structured document model backing the editor, Loom, UserManual, and projections.
constraints: Use the existing microtask_plan_draft execution surface only; do not introduce a separate MT execution surface or suite field.

Core Handshake behavior must be Handshake-native: no outside app, outside server, external daemon, Docker-only path, SQLite fallback, Redis/Valkey cache authority, or sidecar paper as proof or authority.

Allowed external runtime inputs are limited to operator-provided models, GGUF/model files, tensors/safetensors, and CUI portable artifacts where the owning WP explicitly gates them; Tiptap and Monaco are allowed only as bundled/lockfile-governed product libraries integrated into Handshake.

PostgreSQL plus EventLedger is canonical for durable state, receipts, indexing evidence, runtime proof, and validation; generated markdown, wiki pages, HTML, context bundles, debug reports, and UI projections are projections only.
Every implementation handoff must be restartable by a no-context local model using only the WP/MT contract, current Master Spec bundle, product code, tests, and receipts.
Rich documents are PostgreSQL/EventLedger/CRDT-backed structured block documents, not Markdown vault authority.
All rich document state must preserve Raw/Derived/Display separation and stable entity IDs.
scope.expected_behavior: RawDerivedDisplaySeparation leaves a concrete, restartable implementation or activation artifact with PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority, Master Spec anchors, UserManual impact, and validator-readable evidence.
acceptance_criteria: RawDerivedDisplaySeparation is implemented or specified with enough detail for a no-context local model to continue from the MT alone.
The MT names exact product/governance files, state tables or APIs, constraints, failure modes, and verification evidence.
The MT rejects sidecar research-paper authority and outside-runtime proof; accepted research ideas are restated as Handshake-native requirements.
The MT handoff names HBR rows proven, blocked, or not applicable with concrete reasons.
verification: Parse the WP/MT contract and confirm this MT keeps all required fields populated.
Run the smallest relevant product, governance, or fixture test available for the touched surface; if not implemented yet, define the exact future test and negative case.
For UI/editor/UserManual surfaces, use visual debugging or declare why no GUI surface exists for this MT.
Confirm no SQLite, Docker-default, external-app, external-daemon, sidecar-paper, or mock-only proof satisfies the MT.
handoff_requirements: List files changed or intentionally left untouched.
List commands/tests run and exact pass/fail/blocker status.
List EventLedger/PostgreSQL evidence, receipt IDs, fixtures, visual-debug artifacts, and repair queues produced or expected.
List remaining risks, compatibility shims, deprecated wording, and next MTs unblocked.

evidence: Built+verified (commits b506d832 backend core + 935e0482 manual + bf0c62a5 integration tests + dd3056f5 frontend); RichDocumentCore: 6 real-PG integration + 27 unit backend tests + 8 vitest (app suite 349/349). 18 block kinds, RawDerivedDisplay (CX-100), stable KBL- ids, save/load API w/ receipts, projections (negative proof: projection-delete leaves authority byte-identical), embeds (absolute paths DB-CHECK rejected), backlinks (stable KDLNK- relationship_id), history, permissions, crash recovery; migrations 0280-0282; RichDocumentView.tsx wired to real authority with stable selectors for MT-161+ GUI groups. UserManual coverage added (1 group + 10 cmd refs).

adversarial_review_v2.note: Raw/Derived/Display separation clean: raw verbatim authority, derived recomputed on every parse, display preserved from attrs.display only (block_tree.rs:169-209,360-389).

wp_validator_review_v2.review_method: Integration Validator multi-agent workflow (wf_0ac25815-485 + wf_98386044-70a): independent per-MT reviewer + adversarial confirm on FAIL. No ACP. Governance/repo paperwork non-blocking per operator directive (2026-06-18). Bar = good code vs MT contract + WP packet + current Master Spec v02.194, plus the WP_VALIDATOR Spec-Realism Gate.
wp_validator_review_v2.summary: MT-147 (RawDerivedDisplaySeparation) requires separating raw document authority, regenerable derived summaries, and display-only UI state on every rich-document block, backed by PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority with stable entity IDs. The code delivers exactly this in block_tree.rs (RawDerivedDisplay: raw=verbatim node authority, derived=recomputed-on-parse summary, display=attrs.display-only hints; to_document_json emits raw only; stable KBL- block ids) and proves it through real Handshake-managed PostgreSQL save/reload integration tests plus migrations 0280-0282.
findings: block_tree.rs:187-195 — RawDerivedDisplay struct cleanly separates the three layers: raw=AUTHORITY (canonical ProseMirror node JSON), derived=DerivedBlockSummary (regenerable), display=Value (UI hints only). Doc comments explicitly state derived/display never override raw.
block_tree.rs:296-303 to_document_json() re-serializes by cloning block.content.raw verbatim only — derived and display can never leak into authority, so raw stays canonical on save/round-trip.

block_tree.rs:208-227 DerivedBlockSummary::from_plain_text recomputes plain_text/word_count/preview on every parse (line 386 calls it on each parse_block) — derived is never persisted as authority, satisfying 'recomputed on every parse'.
block_tree.rs:378-383 display is read ONLY from attrs.display and preserved verbatim (empty object if absent); it carries no authority — matches the MT's display-only separation requirement.

block_tree.rs:328-343 + 388-395 stable KBL- block ids: existing attrs.block_id preserved, else derived deterministically from (doc id, length-prefixed injective preimage of position path + raw text). Satisfies MT 'stable entity IDs' constraint. Unit tests at 629-653 prove determinism + preservation.
Unit test raw_derived_display_are_separated (block_tree.rs:607-627) directly asserts raw==verbatim node, derived recomputed (word_count=3), display preserved ({fold:true}).

Real-PG proof: knowledge_document_model_tests.rs:74-127 creates a mixed document in Handshake-managed PostgreSQL, reloads it, parses the typed tree, and asserts MT-147 derived content is regenerable (line 122-127); content_json survives save/reload byte-equal (line 179); display attr present in fixture (line 45). Negative proof (projection-delete leaves authority byte-identical) is part of the same suite per evidence.

knowledge_pg_support.rs:1-24 confirms REAL PostgreSQL only via managed cluster (ManagedPostgres::ensure_running, native no-Docker) or POSTGRES_TEST_URL/DATABASE_URL; explicitly NO SQLite/in-memory/mock fallback — returns None and SKIPs only when PG binaries are genuinely absent, else panics. Sub-rule 2 satisfied.
Migrations 0280-0282 (knowledge_document identity/embeds/backlinks) are committed in b506d832 — real durable schema, not scaffolding.
Spec-Realism sub_rule_3 NA: lifecycle.claimed_by=null, completed_by=null, ready_by=KERNEL_BUILDER set status READY_FOR_VALIDATION (not a self-signed COMPLETED) — handoff is correct.

No reachable placeholders/escapes: the only 'placeholder' hits (embed.rs:9/259, projection.rs:231/548) are the editor's real repairable-embed UI placeholder feature, not todo!/unimplemented!/Unavailable code escapes. Aside (non-blocking): packet cites v02.192 spec anchors while current is v02.194 — expected paperwork drift, not a code defect.
evidence_checked: grep for sqlite/placeholder/todo escapes across knowledge_document/ and tests

minimum_controls: No placeholders, mocks, scaffolding, or deferred-live escapes; runtime proof against Handshake-managed PostgreSQL/EventLedger where the contract names durable state.
